texto: No pain that we suffer, no trial that we experience is wasted. It ministers to our education, to the development of such qualities as patience, faith, fortitude and humility. All that we suffer and all that we endure, especially when we endure it patiently, builds up our characters, purifies our hearts, expands our souls, and makes us more tender and charitable, more worthy to be called the children of God . . . and it is through sorrow and suffering, toil and tribulation, that we gain the education that we come here to acquire and which will make us more like our Father and Mother in heaven.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Orson F. Whitney: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “No pain that we suffer, no trial that we experience is wasted. It ministers to our education, to …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, endurance, experience pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e experience.

texto: The spirit of the gospel is optimistic; it trusts in God and looks on the bright side of things. The opposite or pessimistic spirit drags men down and away from God, looks on the dark side, murmurs, complains, and is slow to yield obedience.
explicacao: Citação de Orson F. Whitney sobre temas ligados a fe. “The spirit of the gospel is optimistic; it trusts in God and looks on the bright side of things. …” articula fe, gospel, optimism em registro de fe, com relevância para contextos como reflexao-noturna e optimism.

texto: What has history said of eminence without honor, wealth without wisdom, power and possessions without principle? The answer is reiterated in the overthrow of the mightiest empires of ancient times. Babylon, Persia, Greece, Rome! The four successive, universal powers of the past. What and where are they?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Orson F. Whitney que provoca passagem do tempo e prioridades. Ao questionar “What has history said of eminence without honor, wealth without wisdom, power and possessions wit…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre sabedoria, power, past, com foco em power e past.
